pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 9 and export_result.sh line 6 both define PROJECT_DIR=/home/ga/legacy_project

Task requirements specify two services named 'app' and 'db'; verified in export_result.sh lines 41-42 which check for 'app' and 'db' services; verifier.py lines 49-50 check app_running and db_running flags
Official Docker Hub repository at https://hub.docker.com/_/postgres confirms the repository name is 'postgres'; setup_task.sh line 133 pre-pulls 'postgres:15-alpine' which uses this repository
app.py line 80 specifies app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=5000); task requirements state 'accessible at http://localhost:5000'; export_result.sh line 83 verifies app on localhost:5000

privileged_info_summary: The task sets up a legacy Flask application in /home/ga/legacy_project that agents must refactor into microservices. The application uses Flask 3.0.0 and psycopg2-binary 2.9.9 from PyPI (real packages with real versions). The expected microservices are named 'app' and 'db'. The database service uses the official Docker postgres image repository (postgres:15-alpine), and the Flask application listens on port 5000. The Flask application is generated synthetically by the task setup but uses real, publicly-available Docker images and Python packages.
